formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a comprehensive formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Physical Education — Psychology in Sports in JSON format:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs",
    "formula": "Physiological needs → Safety needs → Love and belonging needs → Esteem needs → Self-actualization needs",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A pyramid representing human needs in order of priority"
  },
  {
    "name": "Freud's Psychoanalytic Theory",
    "formula": "Id → Ego → Superego",
    "variables": "Id: primitive, instinctual needs; Ego: rational, logical self; Superego: moral, conscience",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Three components of personality according to Freud"
  },
  {
    "name": "Cognitive Dissonance",
    "formula": "A state of tension that occurs when an individual holds two or more conflicting ideas, values, or beliefs",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "An uncomfortable feeling that arises when one's attitudes or behaviors are inconsistent"
  },
  {
    "name": "Attribution Theory",
    "formula": "Internal locus of control (perceived ability to control events) vs. External locus of control (perceived lack of control)",
    "variables": "Internal locus of control: beliefs about personal agency; External locus of control: beliefs about external factors",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A theory explaining how people attribute causes to events"
  },
  {
    "name": "Social Learning Theory (SLT)",
    "formula": "Observation → Attention → Retention → Reproduction → Motivation",
    "variables": "Observation: watching behavior; Attention: focusing on behavior; Retention: remembering behavior; Reproduction: imitating behavior; Motivation: repeating behavior",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A theory explaining how people learn new behaviors through observation and imitation"
  },
  {
    "name": "Self-Efficacy",
    "formula": "The belief in one's own ability to succeed in specific situations or tasks",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A concept explaining an individual's confidence in their abilities"
  },
  {
    "name": "Motivation Theories",
    "formula": "Intrinsic motivation (motivation from within) vs. Extrinsic motivation (motivation from external factors)",
    "variables": "Intrinsic motivation: personal interest and enjoyment; Extrinsic motivation: external rewards or recognition",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Theories explaining why people engage in activities and behaviors"
  },
  {
    "name": "Goal-Setting Theory",
    "formula": "Specific, difficult, and attainable goals lead to higher motivation and performance",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A theory explaining the importance of goal-setting in motivation and performance"
  }
]
```

This list includes key concepts and theories from the psychology of sports, with the primary focus on the CBSE Class 11 Physical Education curriculum.
